svg.text: The 1R Concept — Risk as the Universal Unit
1R = your max loss per trade. Every win and loss is measured in multiples of 1R.

g.text: Setup: NQ long at 21,450 · stop at 21,430 (20 pts × $20/pt = $400 risk per contract)
Position size: $500 ÷ $400 = 1.25 contracts → ROUND DOWN to 1 contract
Outcome thinking: "this trade is risking 1R to make 3R"
→ Compares trades across accounts of different sizes (1R is always proportional)
→ Forces you to define stop BEFORE entry — sizing depends on stop distance
→ Lets you calculate expectancy: win% × avg R won - loss% × avg R lost
→ Makes losing trades neutral: "lost 1R" not "lost $487 on NVDA" (emotion-free)
Goal: 6-8 months of consistent 1R sizing before considering anti-martingale scaling
